title: lilium speciosum f. roseum
intro: Lilium speciosum f. roseum, also known as the 'Rose Formed Lily', is a variety of the lily plant native to Japan, characterized by its stunning rose-colored flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Japanese herbalism, and is valued for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Lilium speciosum f. roseum include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and bulbs are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
